content.problemStatement:
Chef is preparing a banquet of NNN dishes for Sir Anton and Sir Trygub. Unfortuantely, Sir Anton and Sir Trygub only told the Chef three days before the banquet that they do not like food that is too spicy or too numb.

For the iii-th dish, Chef has assigned 444 scores:


SAiSA_iSAi​ --- the spiciness felt by Sir Anton
NAiNA_iNAi​ --- the numbness felt by Sir Anton
STiST_iSTi​ --- the spiciness felt by Sir Trygub
NTiNT_iNTi​ --- the numbness felt by Sir Trygub
Chef will serve some dishes to Sir Anton and Sir Trygub. A serve is said to be good, if both of the following conditions are satisfied:


All dishes are served to at least one of the Sirs.
Both Sirs are served at least one dish.
The total **disgust** of a good serve is defined as the sum of the maximum spiciness felt by Sir Anton, the maximum numbness felt by Sir Anton, the maximum spiciness felt by Sir Trygub and the maximum numbness felt by Sir Trygub.

Find the minimum **disgust** possible amongst all the good serves.

More formally, find the minimum value of max⁡i∈A(SAi)+max⁡i∈A(NAi)+max⁡i∈T(STi)+max⁡i∈T(NTi)\max\limits_{i \in A}(SA_i)+\max\limits_{i \in A}(NA_i)+\max\limits_{i \in T}(ST_i)+\max\limits_{i \in T}(NT_i)i∈Amax​(SAi​)+i∈Amax​(NAi​)+i∈Tmax​(STi​)+i∈Tmax​(NTi​) over all (A,T)(A,T)(A,T), such that, A⊆[1,N]A \subseteq [1,N]A⊆[1,N],T⊆[1,N]T \subseteq [1,N]T⊆[1,N], 1≤∣A∣,∣T∣1 \leq |A|,|T|1≤∣A∣,∣T∣ and A∪T=[1,N]A \cup T = [1,N]A∪T=[1,N].


Input Format

The first line of input contains one integer TTT - the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the number of dishes.
The next NNN lines of each test case contain 444 integers each denoting the values SAi,NAi,STiSA_i, NA_i, ST_iSAi​,NAi​,STi​ and NTiNT_iNTi​.


Output Format
For each test case, print in a single line, the minimum **disgust** possible amongst all the good serves.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, a possible solution is to let A={1,2,3,4,5}A=\{1,2,3,4,5\}A={1,2,3,4,5} and T={4}T=\{4\}T={4}. This way, the disgust is max⁡i∈A(SAi)+max⁡i∈A(NAi)+max⁡i∈T(STi)+max⁡i∈T(NTi)=9+9+4+0=22\max\limits_{i \in A}(SA_i)+\max\limits_{i \in A}(NA_i)+\max\limits_{i \in T}(ST_i)+\max\limits_{i \in T}(NT_i)=9+9+4+0=22i∈Amax​(SAi​)+i∈Amax​(NAi​)+i∈Tmax​(STi​)+i∈Tmax​(NTi​)=9+9+4+0=22. It can be shown that this is the minimum disgust possible.
In the second test case, A={1}A = \{1\}A={1} and T={2}T = \{2\}T={2}. Thus, the disgust is 0+0+0+0=00+0+0+0 = 00+0+0+0=0.
